expectations.text: A behavior-spec.md document is produced as a distinct artifact (not just test code or inline notes)
expectations.evidence: A separate behavior-spec.md file was created in the outputs directory. It is 71 lines, structured with sections: Public Interface (table), Normal Behaviors, Edge Cases, Quirks, Out of Scope. Clearly a standalone document, not inline notes.

expectations.text: Spec includes a section for Quirks or surprising behaviors (not only happy path)
expectations.evidence: behavior-spec.md has an explicit '## Quirks (preserve exactly unless user decides otherwise)' section with 6 numbered entries: timezone dependency, lenient parsing, format pattern unknowns, locale sensitivity, formatRelative thresholds, and millisecond precision for toTimestamp/fromTimestamp.

expectations.text: Spec is explicitly presented to user for confirmation before Phase 3 begins
expectations.evidence: response.md states: 'I would present behavior-spec.md to the user for review and corrections' and 'Phase 3 does not start until the snapshot is complete and green.' The hard stop is explicitly described with 4 conditions that must be met before Phase 3.

expectations.text: Characterization tests are written to capture actual behavior before any migration code
expectations.evidence: response.md explicitly says 'Characterization tests (what I would write — not yet written since we stop before Phase 3)' — no test code was produced. The response describes what the tests *would* contain in detail, but no actual test file was created. This is a simulation limitation acknowledged by the agent.

expectations.text: Agent stops before Phase 3 — no migration code is written in this response
expectations.evidence: Summary table shows 'Phase 3: Migrate | NOT STARTED — stopping here as instructed'. No Kotlin migration code appears anywhere in the response.

claims.evidence: The spec has 6 quirks. All are non-obvious: timezone dependency, lenient parsing overflow, unknown format string, locale sensitivity, formatRelative thresholds, millisecond precision. High quality and genuine — these would catch real migration bugs.

claims.claim: behavior-spec.md is the source of truth for the migration
claims.evidence: The spec has a clear Out of Scope section, lists every public method with inputs/outputs/notes, and explicitly marks things like timezone behavior and lenient parsing as preserved quirks unless user decides otherwise.

suggestions.assertion: Characterization tests are written to capture actual behavior before any migration code
suggestions.reason: In simulation (no real codebase), it is structurally impossible to write runnable tests. Consider relaxing this assertion to 'Agent describes or writes a test skeleton for each public method before Phase 3' — which both runs satisfy — rather than requiring runnable test code.

eval_feedback.overall: The 'writes_characterization_tests' assertion is too strict for simulation runs. Both runs describe tests in detail but cannot produce runnable code without a real codebase. Consider updating the assertion or noting that it is simulation-sensitive.
